message.text: Zip Slip vulnerability in POST /api/file/extract. `zipfile.extractall(extract_dir)` is called without validating that archive entry paths do not contain directory traversal sequences (e.g., `../../etc/passwd`). A malicious zip file can write arbitrary files outside the intended extract directory, achieving remote code execution or overwriting critical system files. Validate each entry's resolved path with `os.path.commonpath()` or use `ZipFile.extract()` with per-entry path sanitization to ensure all extracted files remain within the target directory.

The extract directory path is disclosed in the API response after extraction. This leaks internal server filesystem structure to the client, aiding attackers in crafting targeted path traversal payloads or understanding the deployment layout. Return only a logical identifier or relative path instead of the absolute filesystem path.

POST /api/file/extract endpoint requires no authentication. Any unauthenticated user can upload and extract zip files on the server. Combined with the Zip Slip vulnerability, this allows anonymous remote code execution. Apply authentication middleware and enforce authorization checks before processing file uploads.
